title: Epic 1: Project Scaffolding & Shared Foundations
description: Establish the monorepo structure and core utilities required for cross-package synchronization. This provides the 'bones' of the system. Includes PNPM workspace initialization, tsup configuration, and the shared monotonic clock utility.

title: Story 1.1: Initialize PNPM Monorepo, Build Tooling & Logging Foundation
description:
Establish the base monorepo structure and foundational utilities for testing and logging.
Background: A monorepo is essential for decoupling the core media engine from framework-specific integrations. Comprehensive testing and logging foundations must be laid early to ensure system reliability.
acceptance_criteria:
Root package.json and pnpm-workspace.yaml created.
Directory structure: packages/web2hls, packages/react, apps/example-tanstack, apps/docs.
tsup configured for dual ESM/CJS in library packages.
Shared tsconfig.base.json and .prettierrc established.
Vitest configured at root for workspace-wide testing.
Standardized Logger utility established with support for structured logging, log levels (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR), and pluggable transports.

comments.author: mezmo
comments.text: This story kicks off the implementation phase based on the Architecture Decision Document (architecture.md) and PRD (prd.md). The monorepo structure is designed to support a zero-dependency core engine (web2hls) and a framework-specific layer (react), ensuring maximum flexibility and performance. Note: Use pnpm for all workspace operations to maintain consistent lockfiles.

title: Story 1.2: Implement Shared Monotonic Clock Utility
description:
Create a high-resolution monotonic clock for A/V synchronization.

Background: Accurate timestamping is the foundation of media streaming. Drift between video and audio is the most common cause of poor stream quality.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Package 'shared-clock' created or utility in utils/clock.ts.
- Uses performance.now() for microsecond precision.
- Supports an origin time shared across Main Thread and Web Workers.
- Provides consistent timestamps for both VideoFrame and AudioData.

title: Story 1.3: Define Core API Types & Logging Interfaces
description:
Establish the shared type system for the pipeline, including logging and telemetry contracts.
Background: The PipelineEvent protocol ensures that the Main Thread and Web Worker can communicate reliably. Standardizing logging interfaces here allows all modules to contribute to a unified diagnostic stream.
acceptance_criteria:
Define PipelineEvent interface.
Define StreamingConfig and Telemetry types.
Define PipelineLogLevel and PipelineLogEntry interfaces for structured logging.
Export types for use in both web2hls and @web2hls/react.
Unit tests verify type-safety and serialization of core events.

title: Epic 5: YouTube Platform Integration & Auth
description: Implement the OAuth2 PKCE flow and the YouTube Live API client to manage broadcasts and streams securely in a pure client-side environment.

title: Story 5.1: Implement OAuth2 PKCE Flow & Token Management
description:
Secure client-side YouTube authentication.

Background: PKCE is the security standard for browser-based OAuth2. Storing tokens in sessionStorage ensures they are cleared when the tab is closed.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement OAuth2 PKCE flow.
- abstract TokenStorage interface for extensibility.
- Default to sessionStorage for access/refresh tokens.
- Request minimal scopes (youtube.upload, youtube.readonly).

title: Story 5.2: Implement YouTube Live Resource Client
description:
Manage YouTube Live resources via API.

Background: Automating stream creation removes the need for the user to navigate the YouTube Studio dashboard manually.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Create 'liveBroadcast' and 'liveStream' resources.
- Bind broadcast to stream.
- Handle YouTube API quota limits gracefully.

title: Story 5.3: Implement Broadcast State Transitions
description:
Control broadcast lifecycle states.

Background: Moving from 'testing' to 'live' and finally 'complete' ensures the stream is only visible to the public when ready.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Transition broadcast status (testing -> live -> complete).
- Handle API errors during transitions.
- Expose status to the main orchestrator.

title: Epic 7: React 19 Integration & Developer Experience
description: Expose the pipeline through modern React 19 primitives, including hooks, actions, and telemetry synchronization (useSyncExternalStore).

title: Story 7.1: Implement useStreamPipeline Hook (React 19)
description:
Primary React interface for the pipeline.

Background: React 19 Actions and useActionState simplify handling asynchronous transitions like starting or stopping a stream.

Acceptance Criteria:
- useStreamPipeline hook implementation.
- Wraps pipeline transitions in React 19 Actions.
- Manages 'idle' | 'testing' | 'live' | 'error' states.
- Clean cleanup on component unmount.

title: Story 7.2: Implement useStreamHealth (useSyncExternalStore)
description:
Efficient telemetry synchronization for React.

Background: Standard React state is too slow for 60fps telemetry. useSyncExternalStore allows the UI to stay in sync with the core engine's high-frequency updates efficiently.

Acceptance Criteria:
- useStreamHealth hook implementation.
- Synchronizes with core Telemetry store.
- Provides real-time FPS, bitrate, and drop counts.
- Zero unnecessary re-renders for non-subscribed components.

title: Story 7.3: Create Drop-in UI Components (<OAuthButton />, <StreamCanvas />)
description:
Ready-to-use React components.

Background: Drop-in components drastically reduce the 'Time to First Stream' for developers, making the library highly approachable.

Acceptance Criteria:
- <OAuthButton /> handles PKCE login and logout.
- <StreamCanvas /> provides a ref-managed canvas optimized for capture.
- <StreamHealth /> dashboard component for visualizing telemetry.

title: Story 7.4: Final Polish, Documentation & Example App
description:
Consolidate the project for release.

Background: A library is only as good as its documentation and examples. The TanStack Start example app serves as the ultimate validation of the monorepo.

Acceptance Criteria:
- apps/example-tanstack fully functional.
- API documentation generated via TypeDoc.
- README.md with 'Getting Started' guide.
- Bundle size audit (Core < 30KB, React < 20KB).

title: Epic 4: MPEG-TS Muxing & HLS Segmenting
description: Mux the encoded bitstreams into MPEG-TS packets and organize them into HLS-compliant segments using the Mediabunny library wrapper within the worker.

title: Story 4.1: Integrate Mediabunny Muxer
description:
Wrap Mediabunny for MPEG-TS packetization within the Web Worker.
Background: MPEG-TS is the required container for HLS. Mediabunny handles the complex muxing logic. This must run in the Worker to maintain main thread performance.
acceptance_criteria:
Mediabunny integrated into the worker.
Interleaves EncodedVideoChunk and EncodedAudioChunk.
Outputs raw MPEG-TS packets.
DETAILED LOGGING for interleaving state and muxer buffer health.
UNIT TESTS for muxer integration and packet sequence validation.

title: Story 4.2: Implement PAT/PMT & Keyframe Management
description:
Ensure HLS-compliant segment boundaries.

Background: HLS players require segments to start with a keyframe and valid Program Association/Map Tables (PAT/PMT).

Acceptance Criteria:
- Forces Keyframe generation at segment boundaries.
- Prepends PAT/PMT metadata to every new segment.
- Validates bitstream compliance.

title: Story 4.3: Implement HLS Segmenter
description:
Buffer packets into discrete HLS segments.

Background: Segments (.ts files) are the units of delivery in HLS. Configurable duration (e.g., 2s) balances latency and stability.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Buffers MPEG-TS packets based on time duration.
- Finalizes segments and passes them to the uploader queue.
- Supports configurable segment duration.

title: Epic 3: High-Performance WebCodecs Encoding
description: Integrate the WebCodecs API within a dedicated Web Worker to transform raw frames into H.264/AAC bitstreams. This ensures high-performance encoding without blocking the UI thread and includes fallback profile logic.

title: Story 3.1: Implement Web Worker Encoder Orchestrator
description:
Setup the encoding Web Worker.

Background: Media encoding is CPU-intensive. Moving it to a Web Worker prevents UI jank and ensures a smooth 60fps experience for the user.

Acceptance Criteria:
- encoder.worker.ts implementation.
- Establishes MessageChannel for low-latency communication.
- Handles deserialization of VideoFrame/AudioData objects.
acceptance_criteria:
encoder.worker.ts implementation.
Establishes MessageChannel for low-latency communication.
Handles deserialization of VideoFrame/AudioData objects.
PLANS FOR MUXER INTEGRATION (Epic 4) within the same worker context.
Unit tests for Worker lifecycle and message handling.
Detailed logging for Worker initialization and port connections.

title: Story 3.2: Implement VideoEncoder with H.264 Fallback
description:
Wrap WebCodecs VideoEncoder with fallback logic.

Background: H.264 profile support varies wildly across browsers (especially Safari). Automatic fallback ensures the stream starts even on restricted hardware.

Acceptance Criteria:
- VideoEncoder initialization with hardware acceleration preference.
- Implements 'High' -> 'Main' -> 'Baseline' fallback chain.
- Produces EncodedVideoChunk objects.
acceptance_criteria:
VideoEncoder initialization with hardware acceleration preference.
Implements 'High' -> 'Main' -> 'Baseline' fallback chain.
Produces EncodedVideoChunk objects.
DETAILED LOGGING for encoder initialization, fallback events, and chunk output stats.
UNIT TESTS for fallback logic and profile selection using mock WebCodecs.

title: Story 3.3: Implement AudioEncoder (AAC) & Thermal Monitoring
description:
Wrap WebCodecs AudioEncoder and monitor system health.

Background: AAC is the standard for HLS. Thermal monitoring allows the pipeline to proactively reduce bitrate before the OS throttles the browser.

Acceptance Criteria:
- AudioEncoder implementation for AAC.
- Produces EncodedAudioChunk objects.
- Reports thermal 'critical' or 'fair' states via telemetry.
acceptance_criteria:
AudioEncoder implementation for AAC.
Produces EncodedAudioChunk objects.
Reports thermal 'critical' or 'fair' states via telemetry.
DETAILED LOGGING for audio encoding stats and thermal state changes.
UNIT TESTS for audio chunk production and thermal event propagation.

title: Story 3.4: Implement Aggressive Media Resource Disposal
description:
Enforce strict memory management in the worker.

Background: VideoFrame and AudioData are heavy resources. Failure to .close() them immediately leads to 'Out of Memory' crashes within minutes.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Explicit .close() calls on every processed or skipped frame/chunk.
- Memory leak validation using a 30-minute stress test.
- Stable heap size maintained within ±10%.
acceptance_criteria:
Explicitly call .close() on all VideoFrame and AudioData objects.
Verify zero memory growth over sustained capture/encode sessions.
LEAK DETECTION TESTS (using gc-monitoring or heap snapshots in tests).
DETAILED LOGGING for resource allocation and disposal events.

title: Epic 2: Media Capture & Engine Orchestration
description: Implement the raw media capture loops for Canvas and AudioContext, managed by the framework-agnostic core pipeline. This involves handling high-frequency capture loops and reporting telemetry for frame drops and latency.

title: Story 2.1: Implement Canvas Frame Capture Loop
description:
Develop the high-performance canvas capture loop.

Background: Capture must be fast enough to avoid jitter but smart enough to drop frames when the encoder is struggling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Supports HTML5 Canvas and OffscreenCanvas.
- Produces VideoFrame objects at configurable FPS.
- Implements frame-dropping logic based on encoder backpressure.
- Reports real-time capture FPS and drop counts.
acceptance_criteria:
Supports HTML5 Canvas and OffscreenCanvas.
Produces VideoFrame objects at configurable FPS.
Implements frame-dropping logic based on encoder backpressure.
Reports real-time capture FPS and drop counts.
DETAILED LOGGING for capture timing and dropping decisions.
COMPREHENSIVE UNIT TESTS using mock Canvas sources and timing validation.

title: Story 2.2: Implement Web Audio Capture Loop
description:
Develop the synchronized audio capture loop.

Background: Synthesized audio (Web Audio API) provides higher fidelity than microphone capture and enables pixel-perfect sync with canvas animations.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Captures raw PCM data from an AudioContext.
- Produces AudioData objects.
- Uses the shared monotonic clock for timestamps.

title: Story 2.3: Implement Core StreamingPipeline Orchestrator
description:
Develop the framework-agnostic orchestrator.

Background: The orchestrator is the 'brain' of the system, managing the lifecycle of capture, encoding, and delivery.

Acceptance Criteria:
- StreamingPipeline class implementation.
- Exposes start(), stop(), and pause() methods.
- Aggregates telemetry from all sub-modules.
- Handles initialization of canvas and audio sources.
acceptance_criteria:
StreamingPipeline class implementation.
Exposes start(), stop(), and pause() methods.
Aggregates telemetry and diagnostic logs from all sub-modules.
Handles initialization of canvas and audio sources.
INTEGRATION TESTS with mock capture sources to verify pipeline lifecycle.
DETAILED LOGGING for pipeline state transitions and initialization sequence.

title: Epic 6: Reliable HLS Delivery (Uploader)
description: Implement the sequential HTTP PUT uploader with exponential backoff and retry logic to ensure reliable delivery to YouTube even with network fluctuations.

title: Story 6.1: Implement Sequential HLS PUT Uploader
description:
Reliable segment delivery via HTTP PUT.

Background: HLS ingestion requires segments to be uploaded sequentially. The uploader manages this queue.

Acceptance Criteria:
- HTTP PUT implementation for segment data.
- Ensures strict numerical ordering of uploads.
- Handles ingestion URL configuration.
acceptance_criteria:
Implements sequential HTTP PUT for HLS segments.
Maintains correct segment order and sequence numbers.
DETAILED LOGGING for HTTP status, timing, and response headers for every segment.
INTEGRATION TESTS with a local mock HLS server to verify upload sequencing.

title: Story 6.2: Implement Exponential Backoff & Segment Retries
description:
Resilience against network instability.

Background: Web uploads are prone to transient failures. Exponential backoff prevents overwhelming the connection during recovery.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Exponential backoff strategy for failed PUTs.
- Rolling window of recent segments for retry attempts.
- Maximum retry limit before reporting a fatal error.

title: Story 6.3: Implement Ingestion Health Monitoring
description:
Monitor remote ingestion quality.

Background: The YouTube API provides 'excellent', 'good', or 'poor' health signals that help the user understand their stream's stability.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Query YouTube Live API for ingestion health status.
- Report health status to the main telemetry pipeline.
- Periodic health checks (e.g., every 10 seconds).
